prompt: We're launching next month. Give me the phased plan — what fires pre-launch, at launch, and after, and in what order.
reason: Wants a sequenced pre/launch/post plan (playbook), not the strategic wedge (positioning) or a one-page brief.

prompt: We're shipping a new feature and need to nail what it stands for and how it wins before we write any copy.
reason: Deciding the wedge/enemy/what-it-stands-for is positioning, not a phased playbook or a campaign brief.

prompt: I need the single one-pager the whole team builds the launch against — audience, the one message, channels, and the success metric.
reason: One-page objective/audience/message/channels/metric is a campaign brief, not positioning or a phased playbook.

prompt: Here's a draft LinkedIn post — check it against our voice and flag anything off before I hit publish.
reason: Red-lining one pasted draft before it ships is guardrails-review, not a scored audit of existing published marketing.

prompt: Go through our live homepage and one-pager and tell me how well they hold our strategy — a scored report with a fix list.
reason: Scoring existing published copy against strategy is an audit; guardrails-review red-lines a single incoming draft.

prompt: Give me a set of on-voice pieces to publish — a few social posts plus a short blog intro — that sound like us, not generic filler.
reason: Wants the actual posts + blog intro written (voice-content's exact deliverable), not a posting cadence plan.

prompt: I keep posting to the same three accounts at random. Help me work out what to post where this week.
reason: Planning what-to-post-when across properties is cadence (rotation), not authoring one set of pieces.

prompt: Give me a few value-prop statements for the pricing page to test against each other, each a different bet.
reason: Variants of a value prop to A/B against each other is the ab-tester, not a positioning brief or platform ad copy.

prompt: Show me where we sit versus our three named competitors and which space in the market is still open.
reason: A market map + white-space across several rivals is the positioning map, not a single-competitor battlecard or a positioning brief.

prompt: A rep is about to walk into a deal against Acme tomorrow — give them what they need to win it: objection handling and a comparison.
reason: Sales enablement against one named competitor (win/lose/close, objections) is a battlecard, not a market map.

prompt: Turn this customer interview transcript into a case study, a one-pager, and a few social snippets.
reason: Turning a real customer's raw signal into published proof is the story-builder; persona-generator invents hypothetical buyers.

prompt: Who are the five kinds of buyers for this product — their pains, buying triggers, and objections?
reason: Building distinct hypothetical buyer profiles is the persona-generator; story-builder needs a real customer's signal.

prompt: Write a welcome and nurture flow for people who just signed up for our product.
reason: A lifecycle welcome/nurture flow to an owned list is email-sequence; outbound-writer is cold first-touch to strangers.

prompt: Write a cold first-touch email and a follow-up to a prospect I've never spoken to.
reason: Cold personalized first-touch + follow-up is outbound; email-sequence is a lifecycle flow to an existing audience.

prompt: Give me 5 Meta ad headlines and a few primary-text options to test in the ad set.
reason: Named-platform paid-ad copy (headlines + primary text, char limits) is ad-copy-variants; ab-tester is strategic message bets with hypotheses.

prompt: I have a list of keywords and our site URL — tell me what to write to rank.
reason: Keywords + URL → what-to-write-to-rank is the SEO brief, not a campaign brief or a voice-content set.
